note: Additive tool R&D — does not touch frozen paper evidence. First real-data field measurement of the recall-safe benign-context verifier (--verify-fp).

setup.corpus: fsalzano (real Ethereum contracts, manual human line-level labels)
setup.ground_truth: human labels anchored by category+line; zero circularity

root_cause: LLM reasoned genuine vulns away with defensible-sounding auditor judgments; detected lines sit exactly on vulnerable code (not a line-misalignment artifact).
fix: src/ml/benign_context_verifier.py::verify — LLM is advisory-only (needs_review), can never drop; only the deterministic rule-based benign-pattern match drops. Locked by tests/test_benign_context_verifier.py::TestLLMNeverDrops.
takeaway: A 'recall-safe' prompt is not recall-safe; recall-safety must be structural (remove LLM drop authority), not prompted. Controlled synthetic +28.6pp did not generalize to the field.
